暫時亦都未有實質factchecked證據話RCE （即係透過server hack你）會因為外洩而出現 或者應該話 如果要出現就一早已經出現咗 內文話因為依啲本身識整cheats/hack人電腦嘅人好多時已經一早reverse engineer咗source同VAC嘅code 甚至話係唔難做 （source本身有個嘢叫source sdk 佢哋好容易就可以利用依樣嘢reverse到 而且相關嘅code喺1.5年前其實已經喺某discord群外洩咗 只不過係依家就放咗出公海大家都知）

TLDR 主要都係話 外唔外洩其實都唔會好大影響 最多會令啲人搵exploit容易咗 而依啲資料／code 對於整cheats嘅人嚟講絕對唔係咩新鮮嘢

quote返其中兩個多upvote嘅cm

－This. I don't know what everyone is getting worked up about, this is interesting stuff from a dev point of view, in that you can see exactly how the game was made, but everything else that people are saying is gonna be possible... well, probably it won't be.

For starters, cheat makers already know the game and VAC inside out. It's been decades that Valve has been using HL2 engine and VAC hasn't changed much either. They've got it figured out, and nothing new will come from this leak. At most, you'll get a few exploits come out of this that cheats could leverage, but it's not gonna be easier to make cheats just because of this. If a dev is skilled enough to look into code and figure out how to make a cheat or exploit, they would also be good enough to do it without looking at the source.

The matchmaking rank part is just laughable, because it's 99.99% guaranteed that such a thing is never stored on the client's source, and if it were, it's very likely that it would've been datamined somehow already.

This is still terrible for Valve, mind you. No dev wants their proprietary code out in the open like this, even a half a decade old build. But it's not nearly as bad as it sounds in the practical sense. These leaked materials are very interesting stuff for people interested the area though, it's not every day that we see source code of very recent games out in the open like this, normally what gets released is decades old, so this could be a good opportunity to learn for a lot of people. And hey, maybe VALORANT can be good now :^) (just joke, don't hate).

－Security through obscurity is bad and if anything this will lead to a more secure game on the long term. Cheats are already easy to code and all the relevant code paths have been known/reverse engineered for years.

All the important bits are almost certainly server sided and not included in this code.

Also the Source 2013 SDK is mostly the exact same code as this leak so there's not much of new information here in the first place.

官方
Valve Confirms Leaked CS:GO Code, But Has 'Not Found Any Reason for Players to Be Alarmed'

https://www.ign.com/articles/valve-counter-strike-source-code-leak-no-danger

CSGO 官方Twitter tweet咗一篇文：
https://twitter.com/CSGO/status/1253075594901774336

We have reviewed the leaked code and believe it to be a reposting of a limited CS:GO engine code depot released to partners in late 2017, and originally leaked in 2018. From this review, we have not found any reason for players to be alarmed or avoid the current builds.

大意：
我們已經審視了被洩漏的代碼，並相信這應該是在2017年末發佈，並於2018年中洩漏的一部份、有限的CS:GO引擎代碼。就此審查結果，我們認為沒有理由去阻止、警告玩家在最近的版本上進行遊玩。
update: CSGO好似安全
